query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "mint: managed infrastructure for training and serving millions of llms" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it addresses the core mechanism of "Managed Infrastructure for Training and Serving Millions of LLMs" by proposing a specific architectural transition (GQA-to-MLA) and training pipeline to solve the KV-cache memory bottleneck, which is a fundamental infrastructure challenge for scaling LLM serving concurrency.
